title: GTM Strategy/Operations Associate - Dublin
description: Datadog’s EMEA GTM Strategy \& Operations team is responsible for both strategic planning and day‑to‑day operational excellence across the region. The team uncovers, analyzes, and prioritizes improvement opportunities that drive incremental revenue and operational efficiency. We build business cases for EMEA investment, optimize resources during annual planning, and collaborate closely with global teams to enhance systems, tools, programs, and processes. We also lead the execution of global initiatives within EMEA and share best practices across regions to accelerate global scale. As part of the EMEA GTM Strategy \& Operations team, you’ll work with sales leaders and global partners to improve how we plan, execute, and automate core GTM processes across the region. You’ll be AI‑native in how you work, using modern AI tools to get to insight faster, diagnose root causes, and free your time for driving change and partnering with the business. *At Datadog, we place value in our office culture – the relationships and collaboration it builds, and the creativity it brings to the table. We operate as a hybrid workplace to ensure our Datadogs can create a work‑life harmony that best fits them.* **What You’ll Do** * Build strong relationships with local, regional, and global cross‑functional stakeholders to enhance GTM strategy and operations for EMEA, sharing learnings and best practices across markets. * Localize and execute global GTM policies and sales programs (for example: pipeline generation programs, product plays, territory deep dives), ensuring they are adapted effectively to EMEA market realities. * Act as a primary point of contact for a defined set of sales leaders on strategic and operational questions (e.g. account coverage, rules of engagement, data led operating cadence, QBR inputs etc..). * Orchestrate and support the rhythm of business, including data preparation and standardization for Quarterly Business Reviews (QBRs), forecast/LQSR cadences, and annual planning cycles. * Partner with systems, data, and central operations teams to simplify and automate recurring work (for example: standard QBR/LQSR kits, self‑serve lists and dashboards, queue and SLA reporting), using AI‑enabled workflows where it meaningfully reduces manual effort. * Produce analyses, reports, and recommendations on sales performance, coverage, pipeline, and program effectiveness, leveraging AI tools to explore data faster, spot patterns, and get to root causes so you can focus more time on driving action with stakeholders. * Manage cross‑functional projects from initiation to completion, ensuring alignment with strategic objectives, clear timelines, and well‑defined success metrics. * Document processes, definitions, and best practices so that outputs are scalable, repeatable, and easy for sales leaders and partner teams to adopt. Who You Are * 2–4\+ years of experience in GTM Strategy \& Operations, management consulting, banking, analytics, or a similar analytical field, ideally within SaaS or high‑growth B2B technology. * Experience working with go‑to‑market teams (e.g. Sales, CS, Marketing) and comfort acting as a trusted advisor to commercial leadership. * Strong analytical skills and ability to translate data into clear insights and practical recommendations that improve business performance. * Comfort using modern AI tools as part of your day‑to‑day workflow (for example, to accelerate analysis, draft and refine narratives, or prototype simple automations), and curiosity to experiment with new capabilities. * Familiarity with Salesforce and BI tools (e.g. Tableau, Metabase, or similar); experience with SQL or a willingness to learn on the job. * High attention to detail, strong organizational skills, and the ability to manage multiple priorities and stakeholders in a fast‑moving environment. * Comfortable operating in ambiguity and shifting between big‑picture strategic thinking and hands‑on execution. * Effective verbal and written communicator who can build alignment and influence cross‑functional partners. **About Datadog:** Datadog (NASDAQ: DDOG) is a global SaaS business, delivering a rare combination of growth and profitability. We are on a mission to break down silos and solve complexity in the cloud age by enabling digital transformation, cloud migration, and infrastructure monitoring of our customers’ entire technology stacks. Built by engineers, for engineers, Datadog is used by organizations of all sizes across a wide range of industries. Together, we champion professional development, diversity of thought, innovation, and work excellence to empower continuous growth. Join the pack and become part of a collaborative, pragmatic, and thoughtful people\-first community where we solve tough problems, take smart risks, and celebrate one another. Learn more about \#DatadogLife on Instagram, LinkedIn, and Datadog Learning Center. **Equal Opportunity at Datadog:** Datadog is proud to offer equal employment opportunity to everyone regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity, veteran status, and other characteristics protected by law. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. Here are our Candidate Legal Notices for your reference. Datadog endeavors to make our Careers Page accessible to all users. If you would like to contact us regarding the accessibility of our website or need assistance completing the application process, please complete this form. This form is for accommodation requests only and cannot be used to inquire about the status of applications. **Privacy and AI Guidelines:** Any information you submit to Datadog as part of your application will be processed in accordance with Datadog’s Applicant and Candidate Privacy Notice. For information on our AI policy, please visit Interviewing at Datadog AI Guidelines.

title: Graduate - HR & Analytics
description: We are partnering with a leading financial services organisation to hire an HR Graduate to join their Dublin team. This is an excellent opportunity for a recent graduate or early\-career professional with strong technical and analytical skills, who is interested in building a career in HR, analytics, and systems. **Salary:** €35,000\-40,000 **Location:** Dublin 4 **About the Role** This role sits within a small, collaborative HR team and offers broad exposure to the full employee life cycle. You’ll support day\-to\-day HR operations while also working with data, systems, and reporting to help improve how HR delivers value to the business. It’s an ideal role for someone who enjoys working with **Excel, data, and technology** , and wants to apply those skills in a people\-focused environment. **What You’ll Do** * Support HR administration across the employee life cycle * Maintain accurate data in HR systems and ensure data quality * Assist with recruitment coordination and onboarding * Support payroll and benefits administration * Prepare reports, dashboards, and presentations (including board materials) * Analyse HR data to identify trends and support decision\-making * Contribute to improving and automating HR processes **What We’re Looking For** * A recent graduate (or 0–2 years’ experience) in HR, Business, Analytics, or a related field * Strong technical skills – particularly **Excel (pivot tables)** ; exposure to Power BI or similar tools is a plus * Interest in HR, people analytics, or HR systems * Strong attention to detail and organisational skills * Confident communication skills, including presentations * Proactive, curious, and eager to learn *By applying to this job, your CV will be assessed by a consultant from Recruiters.ie for the position described and your CV may be added to our secure applicant tracking system however your details will never be shared to any other third party without your consent.*

title: Graduate - Provisioning and Valuations - based in Luxembourg
description: **This position is based at our Luxembourg headquarters and requires regular office presence.** The EIB offers you the opportunity to live and work in a truly international and multi\-cultural environment. We also offer relocation support. The **EIB** , the European Union's bank, in the framework of its GRAD programme, is seeking to recruit for its Portfolio Management and Monitoring Directorate (PMM) \- Transaction Management Department (TM) \- Provisioning and Valuations Unit (PROVAL), at its headquarters in Luxembourg, two **Graduates – Provisioning and Valuations\*.** *\*Internal benchmark GRAD* **These are full\-time temporary positions at grade GRAD.** **The term of the contracts will be until December 31st, 2028** *Panel interviews are anticipated for May/June 2026\.* Purpose As a Graduate in the PROVAL Unit, you will be mentored by seasoned valuation and provisioning professionals, gain exposure to a wide range of transactions spanning Corporate Finance, Venture Debt and Equity, and develop the technical skillset to grow into a confident finance professional, all while contributing to projects with real impact across Europe. Under the supervision of the Head of Unit and experienced Valuation Officers, you will contribute to various tasks related to the preparation and timely delivery of (i) adequate provisions in respect of the Bank’s loan\-graded portfolio and (ii) regular valuations of the Venture Debt investments portfolio. You will also contribute to the management of data and the maintenance of its quality and support the Team in periodic performance reviews and performance reporting. You will assist colleagues from the Bank in transaction lifecycle management (relationship management, monitoring, coverage, events resolution) on matters related to data valuations and provisions. You may also contribute to the regular valuations of Fund and Co\-Investments portfolio. Specific Post Environment PMM monitors counterparts and financial contracts and acts on contractual events under its responsibility, as well as on distressed transactions, to protect the EIB’s financial interest and reputation. The Directorate is responsible for the financial monitoring, event resolution, divestment and restructuring of EU and non\-EU counterparts within its loan\-graded and equity type portfolios. The PROVAL Unit operates in line with the Bank’s audited valuation tools and the Bank’s valuation and provisioning procedures and policies. The Unit is primarily responsible for the timely delivery of (i) regular valuations aligned with International Financial Reporting Standards for Venture Debt, direct, and indirect (Fund) Equity positions and (ii) the determination of adequate provisions for the EIB’s corporate, project finance and other debt and debt\-type operations, for the purpose of recording and disclosure in EIB’s own or mandators’ financial statements. Operating Network Reporting to the Head of Unit and/or experienced team members, you will cooperate regularly with Portfolio Management and Restructuring Officers within the Transaction Management Department as well as with counterparts in PMM/Counterparty Management Department, in the Bank’s Origination teams (OPS in the EU and GLO outside the EU), as well as in the Finance Directorate, Financial Control and Risk Management. Externally, you will maintain contact with auditors, consultants and other provisioning, valuation and reporting stakeholders as applicable. On occasion, you may also maintain contact with counterparts, other transaction stakeholders, and co\-investors. Accountabilities * Assist more senior colleagues in ensuring consistency in impairment testing, and specific provisions and valuations prepared by the Bank, performing reviews of the underlying documentation, technically assisting, testing, inputting and challenging underlying model assumptions. * Analyse financial statements and prospective financial information and develop and/or update financial models used by the Team for the purpose of provisioning or valuation exercises. * Provide support to the PROVAL Team in managing (internal and external) audit process. * Under the supervision of senior colleagues, interface with (i) customers / intermediaries and (ii) with other Services of the Bank in a timely and efficient manner to ensure receipt of information needed for the provisioning or valuation exercise. * Support the PROVAL Team in the timely collection of information and data such as, but not limited to, financial statements, market data, valuation research, earnings reports, contracts, operational reports and any other data or information required to prepare / update company or fund valuations and specific provisions. * Support the PROVAL Team in (i) executing data quality controls and (ii) ensuring a robust control environment is maintained and that all operational and compliance risks are actively managed and mitigated. * Contribute to portfolio analysis, management information and approval of provisions and related performance and risk reporting. **Qualifications** * University degree (minimum an equivalent to a Bachelor), preferably in Finance, Accounting, Banking, Economics, Business Administration, Management or related fields and supplemented by relevant post\-graduate studies and/or working towards a relevant professional qualification (e.g., ACCA, CFA or CAIA). * A maximum 2 years of professional experience after your most recent graduation (excluding summer jobs and internships shorter than nine months). * Practical experience in valuations, corporate finance, equity research, investment banking, restructuring or the financial audit of fair value or loan provision and impairment assessments would be a strong advantage. * Knowledge of accounting and financial theory and understanding of basic debt and equity structures. * Understanding of the EIB mission and activities as well as the operating and political framework in which the Bank operates. * Knowledge of business intelligence solutions such as Capital IQ, Pitchbook and/or Bloomberg would be an asset. * Proficiency in Microsoft Excel, basic financial modelling skills preferably in managing data sets and three\-statement financial models. Knowledge of Python would be an asset. * Proficiency in other MS Office Tools (Word, Outlook, Powerpoint). Practical experience in using AI tools would be an asset. * Excellent knowledge of English and/or French (\*), with a good knowledge of the other. Knowledge of other EU languages would be an advantage. Competencies **Find Out More About EIB Core Behavioural Skills Here** To find out more about our eligibility criteria click here *(\*) Unless stated explicitly as a required qualification, a good command of French is not a pre\-requisite for hire. As both English and French are however official working languages of the EIB, proficiency in both languages is a pre\-requisite for your future career development. Any language clause in your contract must be fulfilled in order for you to be eligible for a promotion (either via the annual appraisal cycle or via an internal selection process). Proficiency is understood to mean the attainment of level 5 of the Inter Institutional language courses, corresponding to B1\.2 of the Common European Framework of Reference for Languages (CEFRL). The Bank offers appropriate training support.* We hire and value talent with unique characteristics, creating a work environment where they can be themselves. We believe that Diversity, Equity and Inclusion makes us a performing and innovative organisation. We encourage all suitably qualified and eligible candidates to apply regardless of their gender identity/expression, age, racial, ethnic and cultural background, religion and beliefs, sexual orientation, disability or neurodiversity. If you require reasonable accommodation during the recruitment process due to a disability, neurodivergence, or a chronic health condition, please contact the EIB Recruitment team Jobs@eib.org who will manage your request appropriately. By applying for this position, you acknowledge the importance of maintaining the security and integrity of the Information of the EIB Group. In case of selection for the position you agree to comply with all measures (policies, controls, document classification and management) implemented by the EIB Group to prevent unauthorised disclosure of any information or any damage to the EIB Group reputation. Deadline for applications: **11th May 2026**

When you join Arup, you immediately become part of our global community working on some of the world's most recognised projects. **As a Graduate Electrical Engineer at Arup, You Will** * Design of electrical services including electrical distribution networks, standby power, small power, lighting, ICT, security, fire alarm, life safety, vertical transport and various other systems. * Using electrical software packages such as Revit, Amtech and Dialux to undertake modelling and electrical calculations for the above designs. * Preparation of project models, drawings and specifications. * Coordination of work with all members of multi\-disciplinary design teams. * Ensuring that all designs and documents are prepared to the highest standards. title: Graduate Instrument & Electrical Engineer- Immediate Start
description: *Want to influence the power grid of the future? Tackle climate change? Play your part in the greening of the gas network?* *Launch your career with NeoDyne and open a world of opportunities!* **Overview** NeoDyne is a specialist engineering company with deep expertise in Energy Transmission \& Distribution (T\&D), particularly in supporting molecular energy carriers as a vital component of the green energy transformation. We provide high\-integrity electrical and instrumentation solutions across the gas and electricity sectors, with a strong track record in delivering critical infrastructure projects across Ireland, the UK, and beyond. Our work plays a central role in strengthening energy networks\-enhancing their reliability, availability, and resilience while enabling decarbonisation through a balanced mix of conventional and renewable energy sources. Within our **Transmission \& Distribution** team, we deliver mission\-critical design packages that transform the reliability, availability, and efficiency of the energy network, moving it towards a greener, more sustainable future. Department highlights include: * Delivery of high\-complexity design frameworks: NeoDyne is a gas network design framework supplier, playing a significant role in providing electrical and instrumentation engineering services for the on\-shore Natural Gas Industry and Regulated gas utility sector in Ireland and the UK. Our expertise include Compressor Station, AGI Electrical \& Instrumentation design, Fiscal and non\-Fiscal metering, and Cathodic Protection. * Driving complex design delivery: We design AIS/GIS substations from 20kV to 220kV and contestable build and non\-contestable stations in the range of 40 to 550MVA CCGT. Connections include conventional and renewable Power Generation, Peaking, Battery Storage, DS3 and Solar PV plants and large demand loads such as data centres and process plants. With a team of 220\+ skilled engineers, our expertise spans the entire project lifecycle, from design and implementation to commissioning and operations. From our offices in Ireland, the UK, Romania, and the US, we operate on a global scale, combining technical expertise with local delivery. We support customers across Europe, North America, and Asia, delivering tailored engineering services that drive long\-term success. **Position Summary** NeoDyne are recruiting a **Graduate Instrument \& Electrical Engineer** to join our expanding Gas Transmission \& Distribution team. Our graduate programme is all about learning new and developing your existing skills. Over a two\-year period, you will be placed in projects where you get to do meaningful work and contribute to the business. This Graduate opportunity is offered as a full\-time permanent contract, based at our **Dublin Office** , with an excellent salary and benefits package. If successful, you will enjoy NeoDyne's "People First" company culture, built on mutual respect, trust, and honesty, where each individual actively contributes as a valued team member. **What will I be doing?** * Projects could include preparation of coordinated drawings covering all aspects of installations, including mechanical, electrical, instrumentation and structural elements. More specifically, you will be producing Gas Transmission AGI Design including, Electrical Layouts, LV Distribution Design, Control \& Instrumentation Design, Earth Grid Design and General Services Design. * Carrying out Power Systems Electrical Studies: Load Estimates, Protection Coordination, Cable Sizing Calcs. You will have the opportunity to use state\-of\-the\-art engineering software for control and protection design and substation layouts. You will also get to use leading\-edge power system modelling software to run steady state and transient electrical studies, including short circuit studies, protection coordination and grid compliance studies for proposed renewable generation * Generating technical documentation and dealing with customers will also be key parts of our graduate programme to help develop your soft skills. You will be producing specifications for various electrical equipment and assemblies: transformers, RTUs, Gas Analysis equipment, instrumentation etc. * You will also have the opportunity to travel to customer sites to get involved in installation, testing, and commissioning, by participating in design \& commissioning of SCADA and control systems in the field: I/O and instrumentation testing, full testing and commissioning. **What about training?** *Our induction programme revolves around a 70\-20\-10 model for learning and development.* * 70% On\-the\-job Learning: You'll get stuck in from Day 1, working on real projects, delivering real business value to our customers both on\- and off\-site. * 20% Learning from others: You'll be part of a team, working with subject matter experts and interacting with our customers daily. You'll receive support and guidance, and an invaluable opportunity to learn from former graduates who've seen it and done it. * 10% Formal Education:We'll help you become an expert in your chosen field, backed up by formal e\-learning and classroom training. This extends to attaining professional certifications where relevant to our business specialisations. **Required** **Experience \& skills** * Level 8 Degree in Instrumentation/Electrical/ Energy Engineering, or equivalent. * Experience in developing Electrical Schematics using AutoCAD, EPLAN or similar. * Experience in developing equipment layouts using AutoCAD, or similar. * Full Drivers Licence and own transport for site survey works. Desired * 0\-3 years' experience in a similar instrument/electrical design role or Work Placement. * Experience in LV Systems Study software such as Amtech, or similar. **Characteristics** *We look for candidates who* * Work well as part of a team. * Have excellent problem solving and diagnostic skills. * Can complete tasks independently, capable of self\-learning. * Can prioritise and coordinate work. * Have excellent customer service, interpersonal, communication and organisational skills. * Can multitask and work well under pressure. * Are a strategic business thinker. **What's on offer?** * Competitive starting salary with year\-on\-year bonuses and salary increases * Paid Overtime * Paid Graduation Day * Health Insurance * Income Protection * Life Assurance * PRSA pension with employer contribution * Flexible Working * Personalised Career Development Plan * Professional Certifications, pathways to become a Chartered Engineer * Cycle to work scheme **Apply today!** With our diverse range of business specialisations, customers, and industries that we serve, we offer an unrivalled programme designed to accelerate your personal and professional development. Don't miss this opportunity to join a company at the forefront of industrial automation projects in Ireland, the UK, mainland Europe and the US. So why wait? Apply Today! NeoDyne Ltd. is an equal opportunity employer, and all applications will be treated in strictest confidence.

title: Graduate Sales Executive (Hungarian Market)
description: **About Revolut** People deserve more from their money. More visibility, more control, and more freedom. Since 2015, Revolut has been on a mission to deliver just that. Our powerhouse of products — including spending, saving, investing, exchanging, travelling, and more — help our 70\+ million customers get more from their money every day. As we continue our lightning\-fast growth,‌ 2 things are essential to our success: our people and our culture. In recognition of our outstanding employee experience, we've been certified as a Great Place to Work™. So far, we have 13,000\+ people working around the world, from our offices and remotely, to help us achieve our mission. And we're looking for more brilliant people. People who love building great products, redefining success, and turning the complexity of a chaotic world into the simplicity of a beautiful solution. **About The Role** Our Sales team powers customer growth around the world. From prospecting to acquisition and activation, they own their market segments, solve complex problems, and help more customers discover the value of Revolut with a sharp, solution\-oriented approach. We're looking for a Sales Executive to join our Graduate Sales Programme, which offers an unparalleled opportunity for ambitious graduates, regardless of prior experience, to join a high\-performing, international team. You'll be at the forefront of delivering cutting\-edge financial solutions to customers while building key relationships. You’ll kick things off with a focused bootcamp, complete with workshops, lectures, and hands\-on training to sharpen your commercial and communication skills. Once you're up to speed, you'll dive straight into the on\-the\-job experience by working with real clients. Watch this video from our team members about what goes into being an Account Executive at Revolut. Up to shape what's next in finance? Let's get in touch. **What You'll Be Doing** * Outreaching prospects across multiple touch points and channels, with an emphasis on cold\-calling * Developing and executing a robust lead\-sourcing strategy by identifying key verticals, diversifying your prospects base, and maintaining consistent activity levels * Building and managing your own pipeline, from prospecting to closing and account management, maximising adoption and upsell/cross\-sell opportunities * Liaising customer relationships with internal stakeholders to ensure a smooth experience, from getting started to daily usage * Being autonomous to effectively balance your time (prospect vs. close vs. upsell), activate internal resources, and engage your team * Using solution\-based selling to understand customer needs and communicate the value proposition * Becoming a product expert on Revolut Business, its market positioning, and the solutions it offers, backed by relevant, compelling case studies **What You'll Need** * Fluency in English and Hungarian * To have graduated from university in 2024 or 2025 * Excellent communication skills and articulation over the phone * Impeccable literacy skills (email, LinkedIn outreach) * A drive to launch a career in a high\-performing sales culture * An interest in the financial industry **Nice to have** * Previous internship or experience in a high\-performance, fast\-paced environment, with exposure to strategic thinking, analytical tasks, or client\-facing work Building a global financial super app isn’t enough. Our Revoluters are a priority, and that’s why in 2021 we launched our inaugural D\&I Framework, designed to help us thrive and grow everyday. We're not just doing this because it's the right thing to do. We’re doing it because we know that seeking out diverse talent and creating an inclusive workplace is the way to create exceptional, innovative products and services for our customers. That’s why we encourage applications from people with diverse backgrounds and experiences to join this multicultural, hard\-working team. **Important notice for candidates:** Job scams are on the rise. Please keep these guidelines in mind when applying for any open roles. * Only apply through official Revolut channels. We don’t use any third\-party services or platforms for our recruitment. * Always double\-check the emails you receive. Make sure all communications are being done through official Revolut emails, with an @revolut.com domain. **We won't ask for payment or personal financial information during the hiring process.** If anyone does ask you for this, it’s a scam. Report it immediately. By submitting this application, I confirm that all the information given by me in this application for employment and any additional documents attached hereto are true to the best of my knowledge and that I have not wilfully suppressed any material fact. I confirm I have disclosed if applicable any previous employment with Revolut. I accept that if any of the information given by me in this application is in any way false or incorrect, my application may be rejected, any offer of employment may be withdrawn or my employment with Revolut may be terminated summarily or I may be dismissed. By submitting this application, I agree that my personal data will be processed in accordance with Revolut's Candidate Privacy Notice

title: Graduate Structural and Civil Engineer
description: At Rendel Ingerop, we provide client\-focused engineering solutions on major engineering construction projects worldwide in the infrastructure, transportation, energy, and industry sectors. With headquarters in Dublin, we work closely with our parent Company, Rendel Limited, as part of an international multidisciplinary design consultancy which brings a wealth of experience dating back to 1838\. We are also proud to be part of the Ingerop Group, a French Engineering Consultancy with nearly 4,000 employees across Europe, Africa, South America and Australia. We have an exciting opportunity for a **Graduate Structural and Civil Engineer** to join our team in Dublin. **About our role:** This is an exciting opportunity to join a growing Company, recently launched in Dublin, but with the backing of an established Company in the UK and a wider Group in France. With a strong track record of investing in employee development, particularly in supporting employees through Chartership, we are seeking a Graduate Engineer to join the team. You will have the opportunity to work on major infrastructure, transportation and energy projects in Ireland and internationally. Working as part of a small team will provide opportunities and exposure to a broad range of experiences and responsibilities. Some of the day to day tasks will include: * Structural design and analysis at a range of project stages including conceptual, preliminary and detailed design * Deliver engineering solutions across a broad range of structural types, including value engineering to review designs and assist in finding solutions and improvements for clients, checking and analysis of internal and third\-party designs and finite element analysis and reporting on results * Carry out site inspections, investigations and supervision for projects, liaising with designers and contractors to support construction works * Undertake project management and coordination activities, which may include monitoring programmes and budgets, liaising with teams and individuals and reporting progress and updates * Prepare drawings, reports, calculations, analysis and specifications to support submissions to clients **About you:** * First Class/2:1 Masters Degree in a relevant discipline * Basic knowledge of Eurocodes and other relevant national and international industry standards * Basic awareness of forms of contract, construction methodologies and civil engineering procedures * Desire to work in a consultancy environment on large scale infrastructure projects * Excellent problem\-solving abilities, analytical and numeracy skills; highly results orientated * Good interpersonal skills; ability to communicate effectively * Able to work under pressure and ensure deadlines are met whilst maintaining high quality work * Strong team working to contribute to and collaborate with multi\-disciplinary teams * Competent in the use of MS Office programmes and ability to develop competence in use of proprietary structural analysis programmes to suit specific role requirements **About our offer to you:** Our core values of Technical Excellence, Independence, Integrity, Respect, and Versatility underpin our interactions with colleagues and clients and how we impact the environment around us. The collaborative and inclusive culture that we foster provides growth opportunities for our team Through internal committees and recognition programmes, we actively engage our teams in the pursuit of our goals in health and safety, environmental sustainability, knowledge sharing, innovation, and diversity. Working with us, you will enjoy competitive salary and benefits, including profit share scheme, discretionary bonus scheme, and employee assistance programme, continues professional development, which is at the heart of our culture, and an environment where the support and care for the wellbeing of our people is important to everyone **Apply Now** to join \#TeamRendel

title: HR Professional - Payroll Europe
description: At BBH, Partnership is more than a form of ownership—it’s our approach to business and relationships. We know that supporting your professional and personal goals is the best way to help our clients and advance our business. We take that responsibility seriously. With a 200\-year legacy and a shared passion for what’s next, this is the right place to build a fulfilling career. HR Professional \- Payroll Europe. Location: DUBLIN Brown Brothers Harriman Global Payroll team is currently recruiting a Payroll HR Professional to join our EU Payroll Team. This role is responsible for managing payroll activities and working directly with our out\-sourced payroll provider to timely and accurately pay our workforce across multiple key locations. This is including but not restricted to, Poland, Luxembourg, Ireland (Dublin), United Kingdom (London), Switzerland (Zurich). This position processes and verifies the accuracy of all payment processing, as well as tax filings. The leader hired for this role will also assist in researching assessments received from third party vendors and verifying items have been processed accurately in the payroll including garnishments. In addition, this person will be expected to identify and drive continuous improvement opportunities across payroll and some aspects of general HR operations. **Some Of Your Key Responsibilities Include** Payroll Processing and Verification: * Calculate and verify payroll information for both salaried and hourly BBH (permanent, temporary and intern) employees in Krakow, Dublin, London, Luxembourg and Zurich * Preparation of draft reports for the correct processing of payroll inputs * Prepare standard withholdings and deductions from gross pay amounts. Audit paychecks, benefits adjustments, special bonus awards and commissions and ensure they are accurately processed * Complete Quarterly and Annual payroll tasks Balance payroll records and approve tax payments, as required * Collaborate with appointed third\-party vendor to ensure accurate payroll for all BBH employees Payroll Reconciliation \& Reporting: * Perform payroll reconciliation, including pension payments * Resolve monthly payroll errors Complete regulatory reports as needed * Conduct analyses and prepare reports as required * Perform month end reporting procedures to ensure compliance with internal and external procedures and regulations * Support year\-end reconciliation processes **Other** * Identify and drive continuous improvements in payroll * Help educate employee base on their payroll, provide employee support as needed * Support key payroll initiatives **Qualifications** * Experience processing payroll in any or all BBH EU locations (experience with Poland, Zurich, London, Luxembourg and Dublin) ) * Experience coaching and leading others, and guiding junior team members * Experience with both in\-house and outsourced payroll models a plus * Exceptional organization, planning, and communication skills \- detail oriented * Exceptional analytical skills and ability to identify and drive continuous process improvements * Ability to manage multiple, high priority tasks and adjust to shifting priorities, while meeting deadlines * Ability to work both independently and collaborate in a team environment * Experience with payroll and HR systems especially Workday HCM * Knowledge of relevant payroll taxes and legislation a plus * Knowledge of London \& Dublin pension rules a plus * Ability to handle highly sensitive data with confidentiality and integrity * Excellent PC skills, including Microsoft Office Suite and systems interfaces * Experience in the financial services industry is desirable but not required * Fluent in English \& Polish * title: Hydrogen Education Intern
description: Kinia is a non\-profit social enterprise focused on education. We want to make sure future\-focused skills and learning opportunities are available for all children and young people. We believe that we can make that vision a reality by working together. Find out more about us: https://kinia.ie/. Application Closing Date: 20th March, 17:00 pm Irish Time Role Overview We are seeking a Hydrogen Education Intern \[Science and Engineering] to support our new team in driving the Hydrogen Education Programme. This internship offers a unique opportunity to gain experience in hands\-on project work, supporting schools and youth services with learning experiences focused on hydrogen energy technology and STEM education. * Role: Hydrogen Education Intern \[Science and Engineering] * Location: Hybrid (work from home or hub with travel as required) * Contract: Internship (Full Time or Part Time) **Requirements** Responsibilities * Programme Implementation \& Support * Assist in the delivery of Kinia’s Hydrogen Education Programmes nationally, focusing on the activation of skills across formal and informal education sectors. * Support the use of Kinia’s project\-based learning experiences related to Hydrogen Energy Technology in schools and youth services. * Assist in coordinating and collaborating on the rollout of programme\-related events or competitions. * Support the distribution of educational materials and equipment specific to the hydrogen programme. * Educator Support \& Activation * Assist in providing schools and youth services with professional development and upskilling resources. * Help collect and monitor feedback from participants to ensure high standards. * Stakeholder Engagement \& Partnerships * Assist in maintaining relationships with schools, educators, and youth services. * Support coordination efforts with partners, including third\-level institutions and industry stakeholders. * Help promote STEM education and renewable energy awareness through various channels. * Research \& Operations * Research and maintain up\-to\-date knowledge of emerging trends in education and digital learning. * Support the management of project timelines and deliverables for specific programme components. **Requirements** Essential: * Qualifications: Currently enrolled in or recently completed a 3rd Level qualification in Engineering (e.g., Mechanical, Electrical, Energy Systems) or a related Technical/Scientific field. * Experience: Some experience or a strong interest in facilitating science or engineering workshops or events for non\-technical audiences. * Logistics: A full, clean driver’s license (essential for travel to schools/hubs). Desirable: * Irish Language: Proficiency in Irish, or a strong appetite for learning and engaging in bilingual development. Skills and Competencies * Organisation: Strong organisational skills with the ability to manage tasks and deadlines. * Communication: Excellent communication skills; an enthusiastic communicator of renewable energy potential. * Sustainability Focus: Demonstrable interest or project experience in sustainable energy technologies. * Problem Solving: Strong problem\-solving skills and adaptability. * Mindset: An adaptable, "start\-up" mindset, ready for new initiatives. * Collaboration: Ability to work collaboratively within a team and with external partners. **Benefits** Additional Information Other Benefits: * Hybrid\-Friendly Workplace: Kinia supports access to remote working hubs, or our Dublin and Galway offices. * Annual Leave and TOIL: 23 days annual leave (pro\-rata), plus Company Days and a Time Off in Lieu (TOIL) system. * Expense Allowance: Coverage for travel, accommodation, and subsistence required for the role, plus a monthly phone allowance. * EAP: Access to our Employee Assistance Programme. * Learning \& Development: Dedicated support for learning and growth through an annual development plan. Selection Process The selection process may include: * Screening and shortlisting of applications. * A competitive preliminary interview. * A competitive final interview. We endeavour to respond to all applicants within four weeks. If you require reasonable accommodations during the selection process, please let us know. How to Apply: Click here to apply Deadline: 20th April, 17:00 pm Irish Time

title: IT Business Analyst - L&H Underwriting and Claims
description: **Company Description** PartnerRe is a leading, privately owned, multi\-line global reinsurer with a reputation of financial stability and strength, and a commitment to rebuilding businesses and communities after risk events around the world. Our mission is to continue to be a financially stable and predictable business partner, supporting our clients with solutions tailored to their needs, delivering superior returns to our owner, providing rewarding opportunities for our people and making a positive contribution to society. To achieve this, we live by our values of Integrity, Performance, Collaboration, Straightforward Communication and Respect and Care. Core to The PartnerRe Experience is our global nature. No matter where you sit in the company, you are part of something bigger. Our teams represent our truly international mindset, forging connections across office locations and all levels of the organization and building a network of mutual success. The result is a day\-to\-day experience that will allow you to: **Shape your own career** **Grow in a supportive environment** **Connect with a community of experts** **Make an impact** View Home \- PartnerRe Experience e\-book to find out more about what it’s like to work at PartnerRe. **We are always looking for bright, proactive individuals to join our team!** Please follow our Careers page for all updates on new positions. We look forward to receiving your application. **PartnerRe is an equal opportunities employer.** Please join our company LinkedIn Page for all updates on new positions that are coming live. **Compensation And Benefits** The expected base salary range for this position, when hired into our **Toronto** location, is CAD **$93,713 – $114,538** per year. For positions based in other jurisdictions, compensation will be determined in alignment with local regulations, market benchmarks, and internal compensation frameworks. The above range reflects the typical base salary range for candidates hired into this position. The actual salary will vary depending on a number of factors, including job location, experience, qualifications, education/training, skills, market conditions, and alignment with the role’s requirements. In addition to base salary, eligible employees may participate in our annual incentive program, payment under which is at the discretion of PartnerRe and subject to individual and company performance. Eligible employees also have the opportunity to participate in an array of benefits, including medical, dental and vision coverage, a health spending account, a defined contribution pension plan, life insurance, short\- and long\-term disability coverage, etc. **Working Arrangement** PartnerRe operates with a hybrid work approach that balances in‑office collaboration with the flexibility of remote work. Our employees are required to be in the office 3 days a week. Our goal is to create an environment where employees can connect, innovate, and perform at their best. This position is not for an existing vacancy within our organization. PartnerRe is committed to an inclusive, accessible recruitment experience. Reasonable accommodations for candidates with disabilities are available upon request throughout the hiring process. **Job Summary** We are looking for an IT Business Analyst to join our Group IT Life and Health Underwriting and Claims application team and help drive the development and the adoption of software solutions within PartnerRe as well as support the stable IT operations. This role will be based in Dublin OR Toronto, working with our internal business stakeholders (e.g., underwriters, client partners, actuaries, claims managers) and IT teams on the global level to ensure that our IT solutions effectively support the complex business and compliance requirements of our Life and Health reinsurance business. **About The Role** * Manage BAU system operations, application\-specific ITSM processes, security and compliance requests, change requests, and user on/off boarding. * Work with stakeholders to translate business needs into clear, actionable IT requirements. * Drive the design and validation of IT solutions in collaboration with architecture, development, and business teams. * Test and ensure the quality of the developed IT solutions. * Define and oversee test strategies, support UAT, and ensure delivery of high\-quality, user\-centric solutions. * Guide data and reporting initiatives, ensuring accuracy, consistency, and business value. * Identify and evaluate emerging technologies and trends to drive continuous improvement and innovation. **Qualifications** **Technical Skills:** * Proven experience in requirements gathering, process modelling, and solution design for complex IT systems. * Proficiency with BA tools (e.g., Azure DevOps, Visio, Miro, Figma) and agile methodologies (Scrum, SAFe, etc.). * Solid knowledge of data structures, reporting, and analytics relevant to underwriting and claims. * Experience with the IT development platforms such as .Net, Azure Clouds, Appian, etc. * Exposure to AI/ML applications in underwriting or claims automation. **Soft Skills** * Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills in English, with the ability to bridge business and technical teams. * Proactive problem\-solving, resilience in the face of challenges, and an optimistic, action\-oriented mindset **Work Experience** * 5\+ years of experience as a Business Analyst in insurance or reinsurance ideally with a focus on Life \& Health underwriting and/or claims. **Education/Professional Qualifications** * Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Information Technology, Business Administration, Insurance, or a related field. * Scrum Master and ITSM related certification are desired. **Additional Information** PartnerRe provides a working environment in which diversity is valued, where all employees feel welcome, respected, and empowered to perform at their best. We invite you to learn more about D\&I at PartnerRe. Thank you, PartnerRe Hiring Team

title: Integrations Specialist
description: This is an opportunity to play a key role in shaping and delivering critical digital healthcare systems, working at the heart of a growing **EPIC application environment** . As an **HL7 Integration Specialist** , you’ll act as the bridge between **clinical users and technology** , designing and delivering robust integration solutions that enable seamless data flow across complex healthcare ecosystems. We’re looking for someone who combines **deep technical expertise** with the ability to **lead, influence, and collaborate \-** someone who can both deliver hands\-on and guide others. **Location:** Ireland **Working Model:** Flexible hybrid working What You’ll Be Doing * Design, build, test, and support **HL7, FHIR, API, and file\-based integrations** across EHR, ERP, cloud, and third\-party systems * Act as a **trusted advisor** on integration strategy, architecture, and interoperability standards * Lead and support **Epic EMR implementations** , including interfaces, upgrades, and post go\-live optimisation * Translate complex business and clinical requirements into **clear technical solutions** * Take ownership of **complex integration issues** , acting as the key escalation point * Develop and maintain **standards, processes, and documentation** to support scalable delivery * Operate as a **player\-coach** , balancing hands\-on delivery with mentoring and supporting team members * Collaborate with stakeholders across clinical, operational, and technical teams to drive innovation and improvement What You’ll Bring **Essential:** * Strong experience with **HL7 (v2, FHIR, CDA), APIs, and healthcare data exchange** * Hands\-on experience with **integration engines** (e.g. InterSystems, Rhapsody, Cloverleaf, Mirth, BizTalk, Corepoint, Qvera, IBM Integration Bus) * Experience working with **EPIC** * Solid understanding of **integration design, testing, and deployment practices** * Ability to **mentor and support junior team members** * Strong problem\-solving skills and ability to work in a **fast\-paced, 24/7 environment** **Desirable:** * Epic Bridges certification * Experience with **programming/scripting** (e.g. C\#, JavaScript, VBScript, .NET) * Knowledge of **SQL and database technologies** (MSSQL, Oracle) * Familiarity with **integration architecture tools** (e.g. Visio) Why This Role? This is more than just an integration role \- it’s an opportunity to: * Work on **high\-impact healthcare systems** that directly improve patient care * Influence **digital transformation at scale** * Grow your career in a **collaborative, forward\-thinking environment** * Take on a role that blends **technical depth with leadership and strategy**

title: Intern, Software Engineering
description: **Job Requisition ID \#** 25WD91936 **Position Overview** At Autodesk, we’re reimagining what’s possible. Right now, we’re transforming how our products work by moving to the cloud—opening up exciting opportunities for collaboration, innovation, and impact. Along the way, you’ll gain exposure to our exciting tech stack, which includes Apache Kafka, Flink, AWS, Java, Python, APIs and backend development and DevOps tools. **Responsibilities** * Learn and apply cutting\-edge cloud and data streaming technologies * Build and test backend APIs for our cloud and data streaming platform * Prototype new features and contribute to production\-ready components * Collaborate with engineers to design, develop, and maintain scalable systems * Document and share your ideas and solutions with the team * Research and share insights on emerging technologies to help shape future development * Leverage AI vibe coding tools for Innovative solutions **Minimum Qualifications** * Currently pursuing a Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in a STEM field within the EMEA region (ideally in penultimate or final year) * Please state your expected graduation month and year in your application * A growth mindset, with enthusiasm for learning new technologies and tackling challenges proactively * Detailed oriented, and passionate about solving challenging technical problems **The Ideal Candidate** * Experience with Java, and Python * Exposure to AWS, CI/CD and DevOps practices * Familiarity with Git or other version control systems * Understanding of software development principles * Interest in data streaming technologies and backend systems Learn More **About Autodesk** Welcome to Autodesk! We take great pride in our culture here at Autodesk – it’s at the core of everything we do. Our culture guides the way we work and treat each other, informs how we connect with customers and partners, and defines how we show up in the world. Join us! **Salary transparency** Salary is one part of Autodesk’s competitive compensation package. Offers are based on the candidate’s experience, educational level, and geographic location. **Diversity \& Belonging** We take pride in cultivating a culture of belonging where everyone can thrive. title: Junior Platform Engineer
description: **Location** Ireland, Ireland \- Dublin, Ireland \- Kerry **Contract Type** Permanent Full Time **Closing Date**07/05/2026 **The Role** ------------ Fexco's Platform \& Infrastructure Engineering teams build and run the shared services the wider engineering organisation depends on, the paved roads that let engineering teams ship product without infrastructure becoming a bottleneck. We work primarily in Azure, write Terraform, operate Kubernetes, and build the self\-service tooling that ties our service catalog together. Fexco's principle is, you build it, you own it. Platform provides the paved roads; application teams own their applications. Fexco thinks IaC first. If your instinct is to click through a console to fix something, this is not the role for you. This is a Junior Platform Engineer position. You will work alongside mid and senior Platform Engineers on the inner workings of these services, including Kubernetes clusters, Terraform modules, identity and networking, GitOps delivery with GitLab and ArgoCD, observability on Grafana Cloud, and the platform tooling that makes all of it self\-service. We are also building an Internal Developer Platform (IDP), which you will help contribute to as you grow. **This is a Platform Engineering role, not DevOps** --------------------------------------------------- Application teams own their own pipelines and deployments. Your focus is the shared services, tooling, and infrastructure they run on. **Core Responsibilities** ------------------------- * Contribute to well\-scoped components of our multi\-tenant Kubernetes platform on AKS under the guidance of senior Platform Engineers. * Deploy, observe, and help operate workloads on Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS). * Read, extend, and contribute to Terraform configurations using established module patterns. * Write basic Bash and Python automation to reduce manual work and eliminate toil. * Implement pre\-designed network and security controls: network policies, RBAC, identity and access. * Help build and maintain services in the catalog that application teams consume. * Contribute to GitOps delivery using ArgoCD, Helm, and Kustomize. * Write clear documentation and runbooks so platform knowledge stays in the team, not in individuals. * Share on\-call duties with the wider team as you build operational experience. **Essential Skills** -------------------- ### **Cloud** * Some production or near\-production exposure to a major public cloud. Azure is preferred; AWS or GCP experience is also fine. * Familiarity with cloud networking fundamentals: VPCs or VNets, subnets, peering, VPNs. ### **Kubernetes** * Hands\-on experience deploying workloads on Kubernetes (AKS, GKE, EKS, or self\-managed). * Understands core concepts: pods, deployments, services, RBAC, namespaces. ### **Infrastructure as Code** * Comfortable reading and modifying Terraform; understands module composition. * Comfortable writing basic Bash scripts for everyday automation; willing to learn more as needed. * Some Python for tasks beyond simple shell scripts. ### **Version Control and CI/CD** * Git fluency beyond basic commits. * Exposure to CI/CD pipelines (GitLab, GitHub Actions, or similar). ### **Networking and Security** * TCP/IP, DNS, and L2 to L7 fundamentals. * Basic understanding of identity and access control (SAML, OIDC). ### **Mindset** * Infrastructure as Code first. * Learning mindset, takes feedback and runs with it. * Comfortable working in a remote\-first, written communication environment. **Nice to Have** ---------------- * Kubernetes KCNA (Kubernetes and Cloud Native Associate) or equivalent foundational certification. * Experience with Grafana and Prometheus. * Exposure to ArgoCD or other GitOps tooling. * Experience in regulated environments (financial services, healthcare, public sector). * Contributions to open\-source infrastructure or platform tooling. **What Good Looks Like in Year One** ------------------------------------ * Paired effectively with senior engineers and picked up core platform patterns. * Shipped scoped pieces of the platform independently by the end of year one. * Contributed to platform documentation, runbooks, and self\-service tooling. * Built operational confidence through shared on\-call and incident participation. **Note on Travel** ------------------ This role requires occasional travel to our Kerry office for team meetings and collaboration. While day\-to\-day work can be hybrid, you need to be willing to travel to Kerry when needed. **Fexco is an equal opportunities employer and is proud to foster a work environment where our people are supported and encouraged to be themselves. We welcome applicants of all backgrounds and sections of society and each application is given fair consideration.**

and supplier performance **Team Collaboration** * Work closely with Warehouse, Operations, and Quality teams to ensure alignment between purchasing and delivery requirements * Escalate supply risks, shortages, or issues promptly Contribute to continuous improvement within the purchasing function * ***Key Requirements*** * Strong interest in food, fresh produce, or supply chain operations * Excellent organisational and timemanagement skills * Ability to work effectively in a fastpaced environment * Strong attention to detail, particularly with orders, pricing, and quality * Good numerical skills and confidence working with data * Strong communication and teamwork skills * Proficiency in Excel or similar systems * Proactive, flexible, and eager to learn ***Desirable*** * Mercurylla velvollisuutemme on kannustaa ja tukea ihmisiä toteuttamaan visionsa itsestään. paikka heidät toimintamme keskiöön ja tarjoamme haastavia kehitysmahdollisuuksia loistavassa tiimissä tukevassa ympäristössä, joka antaa heille mahdollisuuden saavuttaa täyden potentiaalinsa. **Roolin Keskeiset Vastuut** * Tarkista ja ymmärrä projektisopimusdokumentaatio. * Toimittajien ja alihankkijoiden tiedustelujen valmistelu ja analysointi. * Sopimusasiakirjojen valmistelu ja toimittaminen toimitusketjullemme. * Työn laajuuden ja variaatioiden hinnoittelun muutosten seuranta. * Kuukausittaisten edistymishakemusten valmistelu ja hyväksyminen. * Alihankkijoiden tilien taloudellinen ja sopimusvalvonta. * Sopimusilmoitusten antaminen sekä tappio\- ja kulu\-/kiihtyneiden maksujen korvausvaatimusten arviointi. * Tarjoamme nopeita, tarkkoja, kilpailukykyisiä ja kannattavia kustannusarvioita. * Kuukausittaisten kaupallisten projektiraporttien laatiminen. * Tarjoa johdon edellyttämiä tietoja ja analyysejä ongelmista ja riskeistä. * Projektin kassavirtojen hallinta ja ennustaminen. * Yhteydenpito asiakkaan, pääurakoitsijan ja muiden osastojen kanssa. * Kaikki muut tähän rooliin liittyvät tehtävät. **Rooliin Liittyvät Keskeiset Kriteerit** * Tietoa viestinnän ja ICT:n työtehtävien ja järjestelmien laajuudesta * Akateeminen (tutkinto) pätevyys ja/tai asiaankuuluva kokemus teollisuus * Vähintään 5 vuoden kokemus laadunvalvonnan tai insinöörin tehtävissä ja hyvä kaupallinen kokemus * Laaja ymmärrys alihankinnoista * Etsimme ehdokkaita CSA\- ja MEP\-tieteenaloilta. * Kykyä neuvotella ammattimaisesti asiakkaiden ja alihankkijoiden kanssa * Kokemus Excelin käytöstä on välttämätöntä (SAP\-kokemus katsotaan eduksi) * Erinomaiset analyyttiset ja ongelmanratkaisutaidot * Korkea tarkkuus ja yksityiskohta huomioiminen * Tässä QS\-työpaikassa Isossa\-Britanniassa sinulla olla sujuva englanti sekä kirjallisesti että suullisesti. ***Mercury on tasa\-arvoinen työnantaja*** **.** Apply Now

title: Junior Site Reliability Engineer (SRE)
description: **Launch Your Career as a Graduate Site Reliability Engineer (SRE)** Ready to kick start your career with top tier training, real support, and access to leading global employers? With mthree’s Alumni Graduate Program, you get everything you need to build an exciting career in tech. No training bond. No exit fees. No hidden catches. At mthree, we connect talented graduates with leading global businesses. Our clients include top investment banks and world renowned financial services firms working across investment banking, securities, investment management, and wealth management. We are also expanding into the public sector, luxury fashion, and business services. **Role Overview** As a Site Reliability Engineer or Production Support Analyst, you will help keep critical Front Office applications running smoothly. These systems support pricing, trading, and risk management across multiple product lines. You will work on platforms that process millions of transactions daily, involving trillions of dollars in assets. If working at serious scale excites you, this is for you. This role blends technical problem solving with business impact and gives you real exposure to trading technology and processes. **What You’ll Do** * Work with global teams to support and improve cutting\-edge trading platforms. * Build and troubleshoot complex applications that directly impact the business. * Learn to optimize processes and improve system performance and availability. * Solve real problems and deliver smart, practical solutions. **How the Alumni Program Works** 1. Apply online via this job advert. 2. Complete our assessment process via Hackerearth assessment and our academy 3. Join the **mthree Academy** for 6 week intensive **online/remote** training with other graduates, focusing on areas like Linux, SQL, databases, Python, DevOps and much more 4. Start a 12–24 month placement with one of our global clients, supported by frequent salary increases. 5. Most Alumni convert to permanent employees with the client after the program. **What You Need to Succeed** * **Degree** : Bachelor’s in Computer Science or a related STEM field ( **minimum 2:1** ). * **Passion** : Strong interest in Financial Markets and technology. * **Technical Skills** : * Experience with Object\-Oriented Programming (e.g., Java, Python, C\+\+, C). * Solid SQL database knowledge (e.g., Sybase, DB2, Oracle) for writing queries and analyzing logs. * Experience troubleshooting and debugging applications. * Understanding of the Software Development Lifecycle (SDLC). * **Eligibility** : Authorization to work in the Ireland (visa sponsorship not available). **What You’ll Get** * Instructor led training in small, interactive classes. * Competitive graduate salary with pay increases every 6\-12 months. * Ongoing support and mentorship throughout your placement. * Hands\-on experience with top global firms that gives you a strong career edge. **Diversity and Inclusion at mthree** At mthree, we embrace diversity and create an environment where everyone can thrive. We prioritize inclusion, equality, and well\-being, teamwork, continuous learning, and meaningful impact. You must have the right to work in Ireland by the start of your employment and for the full duration of the training and placement period, which is 26 months.

title: Junior Speaker Producer
description: **About Us** How did Web Summit become, in the words of Forbes, “the best tech conference on the planet”? Meaningful connections. Our tech events are unmissable because we make it easier for the right people to meet and connect. Everyone at Web Summit works towards this goal. And we’re just getting started. We’re always looking to build on the impact we have already made at Web Summit. In the coming years we’ll take Web Summit to new markets, promoting global connectivity, highlighting important issues and connecting global leaders – all while making a positive impact on the environment and communities we encounter. To build a better company, we have to better ourselves. We do that by finding the most ambitious people to work with us. **The Role** Behind every speaker confirmed for Web Summit is a mountain of research, flawless logistics, and relentless follow\-up. As a Junior Speaker Producer, you will be the right hand to our Senior Talent Bookers. This is an apprenticeship in the high\-stakes world of global talent acquisition. You will learn how to identify the next big thing, how to bypass gatekeepers, and how to manage the egos and expectations of the world’s elite. Your goal is to support the team producing big results while absorbing the skills to eventually close your own Tier\-1 deals. **What You Will Achieve** * Target Intelligence: Conduct deep\-dive research on potential speakers, their recent announcements, and their pain points to craft the perfect pitch. * Outreach Support: Draft compelling, high\-level correspondence. You’ll be responsible for the first wave of outreach and keeping the momentum alive in long\-lead threads. * Administrative Excellence: Manage speaker data, track confirmation statuses, and coordinate the complex scheduling requirements of high\-profile offices. * Briefing \& Preparation: Create ‘Speaker Dossiers’ for our team, ensuring that when we get a meeting with a VIP, we know everything about them. * On\-Site Support: During events, you’ll be on the ground, shadowing the Senior Producers and acting as a secondary point of contact for speakers to ensure their experience is world\-class. **Who You Are** * The Ultimate Researcher: You can find the email address or the right Chief of Staff for a recluse billionaire in twenty minutes. You are a digital detective. * Radically Organised: You thrive in the details. While the Senior Producers are out networking, you are ensuring the CRM is updated, the briefs are sharp, and nothing \- absolutely nothing \- falls through the cracks. * Eager \& Scrappy: You don't wait to be told what to do. You see a gap, and you fill it. You have hustle genes, but are currently looking for the mentorship to refine them. * Unflappable: You can handle a fast\-paced environment where priorities shift every hour. You take feedback well and view "not yet" as a challenge, not a defeat. **Skills We Like** * Experience: Fast\-paced environment (PR, talent agency, journalism, events, TV, Radio, or high\-end hospitality). * Communication: Exceptional writing skills. You must be able to sound professional, persuasive, and succinct. * Tech Savvy: Expert at LinkedIn, CRM tools, and finding info in the dark corners of the web. * Curiosity: Willingness to learn, ask questions, and scale skills quickly. * Personality: High Social IQ. You need to be someone people enjoy working with, even under intense pressure. **Benefits And Perks** * Subsidised healthcare, dental, employee assistance programme (EAP) and more. * Pension contribution up to 6%(Irish based only) * A dedicated in\-house L\&D department, with access to workshops, online learning and resources to help you excel in your career development. * Company laptop, generous annual leave and flexible working arrangements. * Our very own Tramway Team, which drives community and morale\-boosting events for employees to participate in. * Annual company get\-togethers, charity days, and monthly wellbeing talks. * Wellness subsidy issued to all employees of €1000 per year **At Web Summit, we embrace diversity and inclusion. We want talent from all walks of life to help us on our journey to digitise the conference sector. Hiring decisions are made purely on experience, skills and the needs of the business.** We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.
